essence: Provides clinical support in workplace health, carrying out assessments, managing cases, and promoting employee wellbeing.

identity.title: Occupational Health Nurse
identity.summary: Delivers clinical assessments, manages workplace health cases, advises on fitness for work, and supports employee wellbeing across organisations.

salary.notes: Highly experienced OH nurses or those working in high‑risk industries can exceed £70k-£85k+.

qualifications: Registered Nurse (NMC) with occupational health training
Knowledge of workplace health risks and legislation
Ability to conduct clinical assessments and case management
Understanding of ergonomics, wellbeing, and return‑to‑work planning

lifestyle.description: Occupational health nurses work across offices, factories, public organisations, and high‑risk industries. They conduct clinical assessments, manage health cases, advise on fitness for work, and support employee wellbeing. The role suits individuals who enjoy clinical practice, communication, and improving workplace health outcomes.

habits: Manages health cases and return‑to‑work plans
Advises on workplace adjustments and wellbeing
Communicates clearly with employees and managers
